questions.question: What is meant by the particulate nature of matter?
questions.answer: It means that all matter is made of extremely small particles.

questions.question: Give one activity that shows matter is made of particles.
questions.answer: Grinding chalk into powder shows that matter can be divided into smaller particles.

questions.question: What happens when sugar dissolves in water?
questions.answer: Sugar particles mix uniformly with water particles.

questions.question: What do spaces between particles of matter indicate?
questions.answer: They indicate that particles of matter have empty spaces between them.

questions.question: What are interparticle forces?
questions.answer: They are forces of attraction that hold particles of matter together.

questions.question: Which state of matter has the strongest interparticle attraction?
questions.answer: Solids.

questions.question: Why do solids have a fixed shape?
questions.answer: Because their particles are tightly packed and strongly attracted.

questions.question: Why do liquids take the shape of their container?
questions.answer: Because liquid particles can move around each other.

questions.question: Why do gases have no definite shape or volume?
questions.answer: Because gas particles move freely and spread in all directions.

questions.question: What property allows gases to be compressed easily?
questions.answer: Large spaces between gas particles.

questions.question: Which instrument is used in the syringe experiment?
questions.answer: A syringe.

questions.question: What is diffusion?
questions.answer: Diffusion is the spreading of particles from one place to another due to their motion.

questions.question: Give one example of diffusion in everyday life.
questions.answer: The smell of perfume spreading in a room.

questions.question: Which chemical is used to demonstrate diffusion in water?
questions.answer: Potassium permanganate.

questions.question: How does temperature affect diffusion?
questions.answer: Higher temperature increases the speed of diffusion.

questions.question: What is melting?
questions.answer: Melting is the process by which a solid changes into a liquid.

questions.question: What is the melting point of ice?
What is boiling point?
questions.answer: It is the temperature at which a liquid changes into gas.

questions.question: What is evaporation?
questions.answer: Evaporation is the slow change of liquid into vapor at temperatures below boiling point.

questions.question: What are fluids?
questions.answer: Liquids and gases that can flow easily are called fluids.

questions.question: Which state of matter shows the fastest diffusion?
questions.answer: Gases.

questions.question: Why do gases have low density?
questions.answer: Because there are large spaces between gas particles.

questions.question: What happens to particles when matter changes from solid to liquid?
questions.answer: Particles move more freely as attractive forces weaken.

questions.question: Why does perfume smell spread quickly in warm air?
questions.answer: Because higher temperature increases particle motion.

questions.question: State one important conclusion of the particle model of matter.
questions.answer: Matter consists of particles that have spaces between them and attract each other.
